title: The aggregator array references an identifier that is not a declared
whatItFlags:
The aggregator array references an identifier that is not a declared
`AssetPathRef` constant in the file (and is not a trust-listed external).
whenItApplies: Every aggregator member that does not match a local export.
whenItDoesNotApply: Identifiers imported from a trust-listed `@dereekb/*` module.
canonicalFix: Either declare the missing `export const <NAME> = ...` or remove the entry from the aggregator.

title: The Angular app root does not exist or is not a directory
whatItFlags: The Angular app root does not exist or is not a directory.
whenItApplies: When `apiDir` does not resolve to an existing directory.
whenItDoesNotApply: Backends that don't host an Angular front-end — the asset validator targets the Angular wiring.
canonicalFix: Pass an `apiDir` that points at the Angular app (e.g. `apps/demo`), not the API app.

title: The component's barrel `src/lib/index.ts` does not re-export `./assets`
whatItFlags: The component's barrel `src/lib/index.ts` does not re-export `./assets`.
whenItApplies: Components whose `src/lib/assets.ts` exists but is not re-exported through the package barrel.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that intentionally hide their asset refs from downstream consumers.
canonicalFix: Add `export * from './assets';` to `<componentDir>/src/lib/index.ts`.

title: An exported asset constant uses a builder callee that is not one of the
whatItFlags:
An exported asset constant uses a builder callee that is not one of the
four known `@dereekb/rxjs` builders.
whenItApplies: Every `export const X = <callee>(...)` whose callee is not `localAsset` / `remoteAsset` / `assetFolder` / `remoteAssetBaseUrl` and not a trusted external.
whenItDoesNotApply: Builders re-exported from a trust-listed `@dereekb/*` module — those go through the trust list.
canonicalFix: Use one of `localAsset`, `remoteAsset`, `assetFolder('foo').asset('bar')`, or `remoteAssetBaseUrl('https://...').asset('x')` from `@dereekb/rxjs`.

title: The component package root does not exist or is not a directory
whatItFlags: The component package root does not exist or is not a directory.
whenItApplies: When `componentDir` does not resolve to an existing directory.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that intentionally don't ship asset refs — pass `--skip asset` to `dbx_app_validate` instead.
canonicalFix: Pass a `componentDir` that points at a real `-firebase` component package (e.g. `components/demo-firebase`).

title: Two exported `AssetPathRef` constants resolve to the same path or URL
whatItFlags: Two exported `AssetPathRef` constants resolve to the same path or URL.
whenItApplies: When more than one constant produces the same `path` (local) or `url` (remote).
whenItDoesNotApply: Constants intentionally aliased — but this defeats discriminating refs by symbol, so almost always a copy-paste.
canonicalFix: Choose distinct paths/URLs or fold the duplicates into a single export.

title: The component does not contain a `src/lib/assets.ts` file
whatItFlags: The component does not contain a `src/lib/assets.ts` file.
whenItApplies: Every `-firebase` component package that should expose an `AssetPathRef` catalog.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that intentionally don't ship local asset refs.
canonicalFix: Add `<componentDir>/src/lib/assets.ts` exporting `AssetPathRef` constants and an `AssetPathRef[]` aggregator.

title: A local asset constant resolves to a path that does not exist under
whatItFlags:
A local asset constant resolves to a path that does not exist under
`<appDir>/src/assets/`.
whenItApplies: Every `localAsset(path)` / `assetFolder('folder').asset('file')` reference whose joined path has no matching file under the Angular app's `src/assets/` directory.
whenItDoesNotApply: Custom `localBaseUrl` configurations passed to `provideDbxAssetLoader` — only the default `/assets/` base is checked.
canonicalFix: Place the file at `<appDir>/src/assets/<path>` so Angular CLI copies it to the build output.

title: `assets.ts` declares no exported `AssetPathRef[]` aggregator constant
whatItFlags: `assets.ts` declares no exported `AssetPathRef[]` aggregator constant.
whenItApplies: Components whose `assets.ts` file exists and parses but has no `: AssetPathRef[]`-annotated export.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that only need a single ref and intentionally omit the aggregator (rare — most apps want a discoverable list).
canonicalFix: Add `export const <PROJECT>_ASSETS: AssetPathRef[] = [...];` listing every exported ref.

title: `provideDbxAssetLoader` is invoked but not imported from `@dereekb/dbx-core`
whatItFlags: `provideDbxAssetLoader` is invoked but not imported from `@dereekb/dbx-core`.
whenItApplies: When the call site exists but the import statement does not name `provideDbxAssetLoader` from `@dereekb/dbx-core`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Re-exports from a workspace-local barrel that proxies `@dereekb/dbx-core` — but those defeat tree-shaking and are discouraged.
canonicalFix: Import `provideDbxAssetLoader` directly from `@dereekb/dbx-core`.

title: The Angular app config does not call `provideDbxAssetLoader(`
whatItFlags: The Angular app config does not call `provideDbxAssetLoader(`.
whenItApplies: Every Angular app whose `src/root.app.config.ts` should register the asset loader provider.
whenItDoesNotApply: Apps that wire the loader from a non-standard config file — extend the scanner if so.
canonicalFix: Add `provideDbxAssetLoader()` (from `@dereekb/dbx-core`) to the providers array in `<appDir>/src/root.app.config.ts`.

title: A `remoteAsset(...)` or `remoteAssetBaseUrl(...)` argument is not an
whatItFlags:
A `remoteAsset(...)` or `remoteAssetBaseUrl(...)` argument is not an
absolute http/https URL.
whenItApplies: Every remote-builder argument that is a string literal but lacks an `http://` or `https://` prefix.
whenItDoesNotApply: Arguments built from variables, template strings, or environment values — those cannot be statically validated.
canonicalFix: Use a literal absolute URL (e.g. `'https://cdn.example.com/data.json'`).

title: The component's barrel `src/lib/index.ts` does not re-export `./assets`
whatItFlags: The component's barrel `src/lib/index.ts` does not re-export `./assets`.
whenItApplies: Components whose `src/lib/assets.ts` exists but isn't re-exported through the package barrel.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that intentionally hide their asset refs from downstream consumers.
canonicalFix: Add `export * from './assets';` to `<componentDir>/src/lib/index.ts`.

title: The component package root does not exist or is not a directory
whatItFlags: The component package root does not exist or is not a directory.
whenItApplies: When `componentDir` does not resolve to an existing directory.
whenItDoesNotApply: Validating an app that doesn't ship its own component package.
canonicalFix: Pass a `componentDir` that points at a real `-firebase` component package.

title: The component does not contain a `src/lib/assets.ts` file
whatItFlags: The component does not contain a `src/lib/assets.ts` file.
whenItApplies: Every `-firebase` component package that should expose an `AssetPathRef` catalog.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that intentionally don't ship asset refs.
canonicalFix: Add `<componentDir>/src/lib/assets.ts` exporting `AssetPathRef` constants.

title: `assets.ts` exports neither an `AssetPathRef` constant nor an
whatItFlags:
`assets.ts` exports neither an `AssetPathRef` constant nor an
`AssetPathRef[]` aggregator.
whenItApplies: Components whose `assets.ts` parses but exports no asset refs.
whenItDoesNotApply: Empty placeholders awaiting first-use scaffolding.
canonicalFix: Use `dbx_asset_scaffold` to add a first ref and aggregator.

title: Sub-collection wiring (8-generic factory + parent-aware
whatItFlags:
Sub-collection wiring (8-generic factory + parent-aware
`getCollection`) without a parent-fixture field on the Params type
— or vice versa.
whenItApplies: Sub-collection archetypes (`sub-collection`, `sub-collection-traversal`).
whenItDoesNotApply: Top-level archetypes (`top-level-simple`, `top-level-with-deps`) — they don't need a parent fixture field.
canonicalFix: Add the parent fixture field to the Params type (named after the parent's `firestoreModelIdentity` short alias).

title: A Fixture forwarder's signature drifts from the Instance method it
whatItFlags:
A Fixture forwarder's signature drifts from the Instance method it
forwards to.
whenItApplies: Forwarders whose parameter list, return type, or async-ness differs from the Instance source.
whenItDoesNotApply: Forwarders that intentionally widen the Instance signature (rare — usually a refactor mismatch).
canonicalFix: Re-run `dbx_model_fixture_forward` to regenerate the forwarder, or hand-edit the Fixture method to match.

title: An Instance method has no Fixture forwarder
whatItFlags: An Instance method has no Fixture forwarder.
whenItApplies: Every public Instance method should have a same-name Fixture forwarder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Static helpers, private/protected methods, and methods that intentionally exist only on Instance.
canonicalFix: Run `dbx_model_fixture_forward apiDir=<apiDir> model=<Model>` to scaffold the missing forwarder.

title: A Fixture forwarder exists but its body re-implements the Instance
whatItFlags:
A Fixture forwarder exists but its body re-implements the Instance
logic instead of delegating via `this.instance.<name>(...)`.
whenItApplies: Forwarders whose body contains anything beyond a delegation call.
whenItDoesNotApply: Forwarders intentionally adding pre/post hooks (rare; prefer encapsulating the hook in an Instance method).
canonicalFix: Replace the forwarder body with `return this.instance.<name>(...args)`.

title: Model / Document generic args drift between the Fixture class, the
whatItFlags:
Model / Document generic args drift between the Fixture class, the
Instance class, and the `modelTestContextFactory` call.
whenItApplies: Every triplet's three generic-bearing positions (Fixture, Instance, factory).
whenItDoesNotApply: Triplets that intentionally use a wider generic on Fixture (rare — almost always a typo or rename drift).
canonicalFix: Align the generics on all three positions; the model and document type names should match exactly.

title: The fixture references a model not in the supplied model registry
whatItFlags: The fixture references a model not in the supplied model registry.
whenItApplies: When a model registry is supplied.
whenItDoesNotApply: Downstream apps without a hooked-up registry — the rule is skipped entirely in that case.
canonicalFix: Either register the model in the workspace's identity registry or remove the fixture if the model is no longer used.

title: The model registry has the model but the fixture file doesn't
whatItFlags:
The model registry has the model but the fixture file doesn't
declare a TestContext triplet for it.
whenItApplies: When a model registry is supplied.
whenItDoesNotApply: Models the app intentionally tests through some other harness (rare).
canonicalFix: Run `dbx_model_fixture_scaffold apiDir=<apiDir> model=<Model>` to add the missing triplet.

title: A parent fixture field's name doesn't match the parent's
whatItFlags:
A parent fixture field's name doesn't match the parent's
`firestoreModelIdentity` short alias (e.g. `sg` for `SchoolGroup`).
whenItApplies: When a model registry is supplied AND the field's parent fixture model is registered.
whenItDoesNotApply: Fields whose parent fixture is intentionally aliased; or runs without a model registry.
canonicalFix: Rename the field on the Params type to the parent's short alias from `firestoreModelIdentity`.

title: A model is missing one of Fixture / Instance / Params / factory
whatItFlags:
A model is missing one of Fixture / Instance / Params / factory
call / singleton — the five-anchor triplet definition is incomplete.
whenItApplies: Every Firestore-model fixture (`kind === 'firestore-model'`) that has at least one of the anchors.
whenItDoesNotApply: Skipped for non-Firestore-model fixture pairs: AuthorizedUser-style fixtures (extending `AuthorizedUserTestContextFixture`/`Instance` or registered via `authorizedUserContextFactory`) and pairs tagged with `@dbxFixtureNotModel`.
canonicalFix: Run `dbx_model_fixture_scaffold apiDir=<apiDir> model=<Model>` to generate the missing pieces; or, if the pair is intentionally not a Firestore model, ensure it extends a recognized framework base or carries `@dbxFixtureNotModel`.

title: The `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface is declared after the first model
whatItFlags: The `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface is declared after the first model.
whenItApplies: Every model file with both an interface and at least one model declaration.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files declaring no models (the interface ordering rule is moot there).
canonicalFix: Move the `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface above the first model declaration.

title: The `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface is missing the collection entry for one of the file's models
whatItFlags: The `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface is missing the collection entry for one of the file's models.
whenItApplies: Every model in the file must have its `<camelName>Collection` (root) or `<camelName>CollectionFactory` + `<camelName>CollectionGroup` (subcollection) entries on the interface.
whenItDoesNotApply: Models intentionally excluded from the group container (rare).
canonicalFix: Add the missing property to the interface declaration.

title: The `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface is declared but not exported
whatItFlags: The `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface is declared but not exported.
whenItApplies: Every group-collections interface declaration.
whenItDoesNotApply: When the interface is intentionally module-private (rare — the model wiring needs it cross-file).
canonicalFix: Add the `export` keyword to the interface declaration.

title: The `<Group>Types` alias is declared after the first model
whatItFlags: The `<Group>Types` alias is declared after the first model.
whenItApplies: Every model file with both a types alias and at least one model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files declaring no models.
canonicalFix: Move the `<Group>Types` alias above the first model.

title: The `<Group>Types` alias is missing a `typeof <identity>` ref for one of the file's models
whatItFlags: The `<Group>Types` alias is missing a `typeof <identity>` ref for one of the file's models.
whenItApplies: Every identity declared in the file must appear in the types union.
whenItDoesNotApply: When the model is intentionally excluded (rare — the union is the canonical type discriminator).
canonicalFix: Add `| typeof <identity>` to the union.

title: The `<Group>Types` alias is declared but not exported
whatItFlags: The `<Group>Types` alias is declared but not exported.
whenItApplies: Every group-types alias.
whenItDoesNotApply: When intentionally module-private (rare — downstream code consumes the union).
canonicalFix: Add `export` to the type alias declaration.

title: The `<Group>Types` alias references an identity that isn't declared in the file
whatItFlags: The `<Group>Types` alias references an identity that isn't declared in the file.
whenItApplies: Every identity ref in the union.
whenItDoesNotApply: When the identity is imported from another file (rare — usually a typo).
canonicalFix: Either declare the identity in the file or remove the orphaned ref.

title: The file is missing its `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface
whatItFlags: The file is missing its `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface.
whenItApplies: Every model file (one declaring `firestoreModelIdentity(...)`) must anchor its `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface above the first model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files in `@dereekb/firebase` itself — those are upstream sources owned by the package.
canonicalFix: Declare `export interface <Group>FirestoreCollections { ... }` above the first `firestoreModelIdentity(...)` call.

title: The file is missing its `<Group>Types` union alias
whatItFlags: The file is missing its `<Group>Types` union alias.
whenItApplies: Every model file must declare a `<Group>Types` union of `typeof <identity>` covering every model in the file.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files in `@dereekb/firebase` itself.
canonicalFix: Add `export type <Group>Types = typeof <Identity1>FirestoreModelIdentity | typeof <Identity2>FirestoreModelIdentity ...`.

title: The collection-type alias kind disagrees with the factory body's `firestoreContext.*` call
whatItFlags: The collection-type alias kind disagrees with the factory body's `firestoreContext.*` call.
whenItApplies: When the type alias declares one shape and the factory implements another (e.g. type says `RootSingleItemFirestoreCollection` but body calls `firestoreCollection`).
whenItDoesNotApply: When the validator can't locate the factory body (rare — emits no rule then).
canonicalFix: Pick one shape and use it consistently — either change the type alias or change the factory body to match.

title: The collection type alias uses the wrong generic shape for the model variant
whatItFlags: The collection type alias uses the wrong generic shape for the model variant.
whenItApplies: The 4-shape taxonomy: `FirestoreCollection<T, D>` for root, `RootSingleItemFirestoreCollection<T, D>` for root-singleton, `FirestoreCollectionWithParent<T, PT, D, PD>` for sub-collection, `SingleItemFirestoreCollection<T, PT, D, PD>` for singleton-sub.
whenItDoesNotApply: Multi-document subcollections that should genuinely use `FirestoreCollectionWithParent` — confirm the factory body calls `firestoreContext.firestoreCollectionWithParent({...})`. If yes, this rule is a false-positive (the validator detected `singleItemFirestoreCollection` somewhere it shouldn't have).
canonicalFix: Match the type alias's generics to the factory body's `firestoreContext.*` call. Run `dbx_explain_rule code="MODEL_COLLECTION_TYPE_WRONG_GENERIC"` for the full taxonomy.

title: The converter constant is declared but not exported
whatItFlags: The converter constant is declared but not exported.
whenItApplies: Every converter constant.
whenItDoesNotApply: When intentionally module-private.
canonicalFix: Add `export` to the converter declaration.

title: The document class's generic type args don't line up with the model identity / interface
whatItFlags: The document class's generic type args don't line up with the model identity / interface.
whenItApplies: Every document class.
whenItDoesNotApply: When generics are intentionally widened (rare).
canonicalFix: Pass `<PascalName>, <PascalName>Document>` (root) or include the parent generics for subcollections.

title: The document class is declared but not exported
whatItFlags: The document class is declared but not exported.
whenItApplies: Every document class.
whenItDoesNotApply: When intentionally module-private.
canonicalFix: Add `export` to the class declaration.

title: The document class is missing the `get modelIdentity()` getter
whatItFlags: The document class is missing the `get modelIdentity()` getter.
whenItApplies: Every document class.
whenItDoesNotApply: Document classes inheriting the getter from a custom abstract base (rare).
canonicalFix: Add `get modelIdentity() { return <identity>; }` returning the matching identity constant.

title: The document class extends an unsupported base class
whatItFlags: The document class extends an unsupported base class.
whenItApplies: Every document class — must extend `AbstractFirestoreDocument` (root) or `AbstractFirestoreDocumentWithParent` (subcollection).
whenItDoesNotApply: Custom document hierarchies (rare; usually a typo).
canonicalFix: Change the `extends` clause to the canonical base class for the model variant.

title: The document's `get modelIdentity()` returns the wrong identity constant
whatItFlags: The document's `get modelIdentity()` returns the wrong identity constant.
whenItApplies: Every document class.
whenItDoesNotApply: When the rename was intentional (rare — usually a copy-paste).
canonicalFix: Update the getter body to return the matching identity constant.

title: A persisted field has no JSDoc description
whatItFlags: A persisted field has no JSDoc description.
whenItApplies: Every persisted field — short field names need a JSDoc description so a reader knows what the field is actually for. The long-name itself is carried by the `@dbxModelVariable` tag, so the JSDoc body just needs a one-line description.
whenItDoesNotApply: Fields whose name is unambiguously self-describing (rare with 1-4 char abbreviations).
canonicalFix: Add `/** <one-line description> *\/` above the field declaration (and append `@dbxModelVariable <longName>` for the canonical long name).

title: A persisted field is missing its `@dbxModelVariable <name>` JSDoc tag
whatItFlags: A persisted field is missing its `@dbxModelVariable <name>` JSDoc tag.
whenItApplies: Every field on a `@dbxModel` interface — the tag carries the human-readable long name the catalog and decoder use when surfacing the field to operators. The long name is the variable name the field would have unabbreviated, written in camelCase (e.g. `uid` → `userUid`, `n` → `name`, `crAt` → `createdAt`).
whenItDoesNotApply: Fields on embedded sub-object interfaces (no `@dbxModel` parent) and fields the project deliberately leaves untagged (rare).
canonicalFix: Append `@dbxModelVariable <longName>` to the field's JSDoc block, where `<longName>` is the field's unabbreviated camelCase variable name.

title: A persisted field's name exceeds the workspace's 5-character convention
whatItFlags: A persisted field's name exceeds the workspace's 5-character convention.
whenItApplies: Every persisted field on a model interface (or sub-object interface). The default limit is 5; override via `dbx-mcp.config.json` `modelValidate.maxFieldNameLength`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Fields whose name is listed in `dbx-mcp.config.json` `modelValidate.ignoredFieldNames`, or fields that compose well-known multi-character abbreviations the project intentionally allows.
canonicalFix: Rename the field to a 1–5 character abbreviation (e.g. `name` → `n`, `userId` → `uid`). To exempt specific names workspace-wide, add them to `modelValidate.ignoredFieldNames` in `dbx-mcp.config.json`.

title: The `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface is missing its `@dbxModelGroup` JSDoc tag
whatItFlags: The `<Group>FirestoreCollections` interface is missing its `@dbxModelGroup` JSDoc tag.
whenItApplies: Every group container — the rich catalog extractor (`scan/extract-models`) requires the tag to register the group, and downstream traversal/referencing depends on that registration.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files in `@dereekb/firebase` itself (upstream-owned sources).
canonicalFix: Add `@dbxModelGroup <Group>` to the JSDoc above the `<Group>FirestoreCollections` declaration.

title: `firestoreModelIdentity(...)` was called with non-string-literal args (or wrong arity)
whatItFlags: `firestoreModelIdentity(...)` was called with non-string-literal args (or wrong arity).
whenItApplies: Every identity call.
whenItDoesNotApply: When the args come from a const (uncommon — the validator can't follow non-literal references).
canonicalFix: Pass `(parentIdentity?, '<modelType>', '<prefix>')` as inline string literals.

title: The model's `firestoreModelIdentity(...)` const is not exported
whatItFlags: The model's `firestoreModelIdentity(...)` const is not exported.
whenItApplies: Every identity constant.
whenItDoesNotApply: When intentionally module-private (rare).
canonicalFix: Add `export` to the identity constant declaration.

title: `firestoreModelIdentity(...)` was found but its corresponding model interface lacks the `@dbxModel` JSDoc tag, leaving the model invisible to the catalog and to downstream traversal/referencing code
whatItFlags: `firestoreModelIdentity(...)` was found but its corresponding model interface lacks the `@dbxModel` JSDoc tag, leaving the model invisible to the catalog and to downstream traversal/referencing code.
whenItApplies: Every `firestoreModelIdentity(...)` call whose `<PascalName>` interface is missing `@dbxModel`. Anchored at the identity line so the fix points at both the interface and the identity.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files in `@dereekb/firebase` itself.
canonicalFix: Add `@dbxModel` to the JSDoc on the `<PascalName>` interface so the catalog and decoder recognise it as a model.

title: The model's data interface is missing its `@dbxModel` JSDoc tag
whatItFlags: The model's data interface is missing its `@dbxModel` JSDoc tag.
whenItApplies: Every `<PascalName>` interface that anchors a `firestoreModelIdentity(...)` model — the rich catalog extractor (`scan/extract-models`) requires the tag to register the model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Embedded sub-object interfaces (no matching identity) and files in `@dereekb/firebase` itself.
canonicalFix: Add `@dbxModel` to the JSDoc above the `<PascalName>` interface declaration.

title: The model's data interface is declared but not exported
whatItFlags: The model's data interface is declared but not exported.
whenItApplies: Every model interface declaration.
whenItDoesNotApply: When intentionally module-private (rare).
canonicalFix: Add `export` to the interface declaration.

title: The model has no `<camelName>FirestoreCollection` (root) or `<camelName>FirestoreCollectionFactory` (subcollection) function
whatItFlags: The model has no `<camelName>FirestoreCollection` (root) or `<camelName>FirestoreCollectionFactory` (subcollection) function.
whenItApplies: Every model.
whenItDoesNotApply: When the function is intentionally inlined (rare).
canonicalFix: Declare the matching factory function.

title: The model has no collection-reference function (`<camelName>CollectionReference` for root or `<camelName>CollectionReferenceFactory` for subcollection)
whatItFlags: The model has no collection-reference function (`<camelName>CollectionReference` for root or `<camelName>CollectionReferenceFactory` for subcollection).
whenItApplies: Every model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Models that intentionally bypass the canonical reference shape.
canonicalFix: Declare the matching reference function next to the converter.

title: The model has no `<PascalName>FirestoreCollection` type alias
whatItFlags: The model has no `<PascalName>FirestoreCollection` type alias.
whenItApplies: Every model.
whenItDoesNotApply: When intentionally re-using a parent type (rare).
canonicalFix: Declare `export type <PascalName>FirestoreCollection = FirestoreCollection<<PascalName>, <PascalName>Document>;`.

title: The model has no `<camelName>Converter` constant
whatItFlags: The model has no `<camelName>Converter` constant.
whenItApplies: Every model in the file.
whenItDoesNotApply: Models intentionally sharing a converter (rare).
canonicalFix: Declare `export const <camelName>Converter = snapshotConverterFunctions<<PascalName>>({ fields: { ... } });`.

title: The model has no matching `<PascalName>Document` class
whatItFlags: The model has no matching `<PascalName>Document` class.
whenItApplies: Every model in the file.
whenItDoesNotApply: Models intentionally extending a shared document class.
canonicalFix: Declare `export class <PascalName>Document extends AbstractFirestoreDocument<...> { ... }`.

title: The model has no matching data interface (`<PascalName>`)
whatItFlags: The model has no matching data interface (`<PascalName>`).
whenItApplies: Every model in the file.
whenItDoesNotApply: Models that intentionally reuse another file's interface (rare).
canonicalFix: Declare `export interface <PascalName> extends FirestoreModelData { ... }`.

title: The model has no matching `<PascalName>Roles` type alias
whatItFlags: The model has no matching `<PascalName>Roles` type alias.
whenItApplies: Every model in the file.
whenItDoesNotApply: Models that intentionally reuse a parent's role union (rare).
canonicalFix: Declare `export type <PascalName>Roles = 'role-a' | 'role-b' | ...`.

title: The model's declarations are out of canonical order
whatItFlags: The model's declarations are out of canonical order.
whenItApplies: Every model — declarations follow the order: identity, interface, rolesType, documentClass, converter, referenceFn, collectionType, collectionFn (root) or … collectionFactoryType, collectionFn, referenceGroupFn, collectionGroupType, collectionGroupFn (subcollection).
whenItDoesNotApply: Files where multiple models intentionally interleave (rare; prefer one-model-per-file).
canonicalFix: Re-order the declarations to match the canonical sequence.

title: The roles type is declared but not exported
whatItFlags: The roles type is declared but not exported.
whenItApplies: Every roles type alias.
whenItDoesNotApply: When intentionally module-private.
canonicalFix: Add `export` to the alias.

title: Subcollection model is missing its `<camelName>FirestoreCollectionFactory` function
whatItFlags: Subcollection model is missing its `<camelName>FirestoreCollectionFactory` function.
whenItApplies: Every subcollection model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Root models.
canonicalFix: Declare `export function <camelName>FirestoreCollectionFactory(...)`.

title: Subcollection model is missing its `<PascalName>FirestoreCollectionFactory` type alias
whatItFlags: Subcollection model is missing its `<PascalName>FirestoreCollectionFactory` type alias.
whenItApplies: Every subcollection model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Root models.
canonicalFix: Declare `export type <PascalName>FirestoreCollectionFactory = FirestoreCollectionWithParentFactory<...>;`.

title: Subcollection model is missing its `<camelName>FirestoreCollectionGroup` function
whatItFlags: Subcollection model is missing its `<camelName>FirestoreCollectionGroup` function.
whenItApplies: Every subcollection model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Root models.
canonicalFix: Declare `export function <camelName>FirestoreCollectionGroup(firestoreContext)`.

title: Subcollection model is missing its `<camelName>CollectionGroup` reference function
whatItFlags: Subcollection model is missing its `<camelName>CollectionGroup` reference function.
whenItApplies: Every subcollection model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Root models — they don't have a collection-group concept.
canonicalFix: Declare `export function <camelName>CollectionGroup(firestoreContext)`.

title: Subcollection model is missing its `<PascalName>FirestoreCollectionGroup` type alias
whatItFlags: Subcollection model is missing its `<PascalName>FirestoreCollectionGroup` type alias.
whenItApplies: Every subcollection model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Root models.
canonicalFix: Declare `export type <PascalName>FirestoreCollectionGroup = FirestoreCollectionGroup<...>;`.

title: Subcollection model is missing its `<camelName>CollectionReferenceFactory` function
whatItFlags: Subcollection model is missing its `<camelName>CollectionReferenceFactory` function.
whenItApplies: Every subcollection model.
whenItDoesNotApply: Root models — they declare `<camelName>CollectionReference` instead.
canonicalFix: Declare `export function <camelName>CollectionReferenceFactory(...)`.

title: A non-null CRUD key is missing the matching abstract member on `<Group>Functions`
whatItFlags: A non-null CRUD key is missing the matching abstract member on `<Group>Functions`.
whenItApplies: Every non-null key of `<Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` that doesn't appear as a member name on the abstract Functions class.
whenItDoesNotApply: Keys covered by an inherited member from a base class (rare — CRUD members are declared per file by convention).
canonicalFix: Add `abstract <key>: ModelFirebaseFunctionMapFunction<<Group>FunctionTypeMap, '<key>'>;` to the class.

title: `<group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` constant is annotated with the wrong generic argument
whatItFlags: `<group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` constant is annotated with the wrong generic argument.
whenItApplies: When the type annotation on the const isn't the matching `<Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` type alias.
whenItDoesNotApply: No known false-positive cases — the generic must point at the local type alias.
canonicalFix: Set the annotation to `<Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` so the call map shape is inferred from the type map.

title: A CRUD config leaf references the bare `*Params` interface without the `[Params, Result]` tuple form
whatItFlags: A CRUD config leaf references the bare `*Params` interface without the `[Params, Result]` tuple form.
whenItApplies: Every CRUD leaf that uses `<key>: <Foo>ParamsType` instead of `<key>: [<Foo>ParamsType, <Foo>Result]`.
whenItDoesNotApply: CRUD entries whose result type is intentionally `void` (rare — convention is to declare `*Result` and use the tuple form).
canonicalFix: Convert the leaf to the tuple form `[<Foo>ParamsType, <Foo>Result]` so the function signature carries the result type.

title: A non-null CRUD key is missing from the runtime `<group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` const
whatItFlags: A non-null CRUD key is missing from the runtime `<group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` const.
whenItApplies: Every non-null key declared on the `<Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` type that doesn't appear as a runtime key on the const.
whenItDoesNotApply: Keys whose runtime entry is contributed by another file (rare — convention is to keep type + const in lockstep).
canonicalFix: Add `<key>: [<Foo>ParamsType, <Foo>Result]` (or the bare `*Params` form) to the const.

title: A `*Params` / `*Result` declaration appears below the Functions block
whatItFlags: A `*Params` / `*Result` declaration appears below the Functions block.
whenItApplies: When a params or result declaration's line number falls after the first Functions-block declaration.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files where the declaration order is intentionally inverted for project-specific reasons.
canonicalFix: Move the params/result declaration above the Functions block so the file reads top-down: types → validators → Functions.

title: The Functions block declarations don't all share the same `<group>` prefix
whatItFlags: The Functions block declarations don't all share the same `<group>` prefix.
whenItApplies: When the inferred group name on the type map, config map, CRUD type/const, function map, or class differs from the file's primary group.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files where the prefix mismatch is intentional (rare — convention requires consistent naming).
canonicalFix: Rename the divergent declaration so every Functions-block export shares the same `<group>` prefix.

title: `<group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` constant is missing from the file
whatItFlags: `<group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` constant is missing from the file.
whenItApplies: Every `<model>.api.ts` file with a Functions block.
whenItDoesNotApply: Non-CRUD api filenames listed in `NON_CRUD_API_BASENAMES`.
canonicalFix: Add `export const <group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig: <Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig = { ... };` mapping each CRUD key to its `[Params, Result]` tuple or `null`.

title: `<Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` type alias is missing from the file
whatItFlags: `<Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` type alias is missing from the file.
whenItApplies: Every `<model>.api.ts` file with a Functions block.
whenItDoesNotApply: Non-CRUD api filenames listed in `NON_CRUD_API_BASENAMES`.
canonicalFix: Add `export type <Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig = ModelFirebaseCrudFunctionConfigMap<<Group>FunctionTypeMap>;` to bind the CRUD shape to the type map.

title: `<group>FunctionMap` constant is missing from the file
whatItFlags: `<group>FunctionMap` constant is missing from the file.
whenItApplies: Every `<model>.api.ts` file with a Functions block.
whenItDoesNotApply: Non-CRUD api filenames listed in `NON_CRUD_API_BASENAMES`.
canonicalFix: Add `export const <group>FunctionMap = callModelFirebaseFunctionMapFactory(<group>FunctionTypeConfigMap, <group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig);` to wire the runtime call map.

title: `<group>FunctionTypeConfigMap` constant is missing from the file
whatItFlags: `<group>FunctionTypeConfigMap` constant is missing from the file.
whenItApplies: Every `<model>.api.ts` file with a Functions block.
whenItDoesNotApply: Non-CRUD api filenames listed in `NON_CRUD_API_BASENAMES`.
canonicalFix: Add `export const <group>FunctionTypeConfigMap: ModelFirebaseFunctionMapTypeConfigMap<<Group>FunctionTypeMap> = { ... };` aligned with the type-map keys.

title: `<group>FunctionTypeMap` type alias is missing from the file
whatItFlags: `<group>FunctionTypeMap` type alias is missing from the file.
whenItApplies: Every `<model>.api.ts` file (other than the non-CRUD basenames listed in `NON_CRUD_API_BASENAMES`).
whenItDoesNotApply: Files matching a `NON_CRUD_API_BASENAMES` entry (e.g. `development.api.ts`) — those short-circuit structural checks.
canonicalFix: Add `export type <Group>FunctionTypeMap = { /* keys *\/ };` at the top of the Functions block.

title: `<Group>Functions` abstract class is missing from the file
whatItFlags: `<Group>Functions` abstract class is missing from the file.
whenItApplies: Every `<model>.api.ts` file with a Functions block.
whenItDoesNotApply: Non-CRUD api filenames listed in `NON_CRUD_API_BASENAMES`.
canonicalFix: Add `export abstract class <Group>Functions implements ModelFirebaseFunctionsConfigMap<<Group>FunctionTypeMap, <Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig> { ... }` with one abstract member per non-null CRUD key.

title: The file is missing the `// MARK: Constants` (or equivalent) section comment
whatItFlags: The file is missing the `// MARK: Constants` (or equivalent) section comment.
whenItApplies: Every CRUD api file expected to declare top-level constants alongside the params/results.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files with no top-level constants — the marker is purely organizational.
canonicalFix: Add a `// MARK: Constants` comment above the constants region so navigation and folding work consistently.

title: The file is missing the `// MARK: Functions` section comment above the Functions block
whatItFlags: The file is missing the `// MARK: Functions` section comment above the Functions block.
whenItApplies: Every CRUD api file with a Functions block.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files matching `NON_CRUD_API_BASENAMES` (no Functions block expected).
canonicalFix: Add a `// MARK: Functions` comment immediately above the Functions block so the file's top-down structure stays scannable.

title: The file is missing the `// MARK: Keys` section comment
whatItFlags: The file is missing the `// MARK: Keys` section comment.
whenItApplies: Every CRUD api file expected to declare key types alongside the params/results.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files with no key declarations — the marker is purely organizational.
canonicalFix: Add a `// MARK: Keys` comment above the keys region so navigation and folding work consistently.

title: A required Functions-block declaration exists but isn't `export`ed
whatItFlags: A required Functions-block declaration exists but isn't `export`ed.
whenItApplies: Any of the six Functions-block exports declared without the `export` keyword.
whenItDoesNotApply: No known false-positive cases — the call map / class are consumed across packages and must be exported.
canonicalFix: Add the `export` keyword to the declaration.

title: `<group>FunctionMap = callModelFirebaseFunctionMapFactory(...)` was called with the wrong argument identifiers
whatItFlags: `<group>FunctionMap = callModelFirebaseFunctionMapFactory(...)` was called with the wrong argument identifiers.
whenItApplies: When the factory call's two argument identifiers don't match `<group>FunctionTypeConfigMap` and `<group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` (in that order).
whenItDoesNotApply: No known false-positive cases — the factory must be passed the file's own config + crud-config consts.
canonicalFix: Pass `<group>FunctionTypeConfigMap` first and `<group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig` second, matching the local declarations.

title: The Functions-block declarations aren't in canonical order
whatItFlags: The Functions-block declarations aren't in canonical order.
whenItApplies: When the actual on-disk order of `functionTypeMap` / `functionTypeConfigMap` / `crudConfigType` / `crudConfigConst` / `functionMap` / `functionsClass` doesn't match the canonical order in `FUNCTIONS_BLOCK_ORDER`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files where the order is intentionally different for project-specific reasons.
canonicalFix: Reorder the Functions-block declarations to match `FUNCTIONS_BLOCK_ORDER`.

title: `<Group>Functions` declares the wrong `implements` clause
whatItFlags: `<Group>Functions` declares the wrong `implements` clause.
whenItApplies: When the first `implements` clause text isn't `ModelFirebaseFunctionsConfigMap<<Group>FunctionTypeMap, <Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig>` (with the local type/const names).
whenItDoesNotApply: No known false-positive cases — the implements clause keeps the abstract members in sync with the CRUD config.
canonicalFix: Replace the implements clause with `ModelFirebaseFunctionsConfigMap<<Group>FunctionTypeMap, <Group>FunctionTypeCrudConfig>`.

title: `<Group>Functions` is declared as `class` rather than `abstract class`
whatItFlags: `<Group>Functions` is declared as `class` rather than `abstract class`.
whenItApplies: When the class declaration is missing the `abstract` modifier.
whenItDoesNotApply: No known false-positive cases — the Functions class is implemented downstream by the API app.
canonicalFix: Add the `abstract` modifier so concrete CRUD members are bound by the consuming `*ServerActions` class.

title: A `.api.ts` file follows the naming convention but is not a CRUD model api
whatItFlags: A `.api.ts` file follows the naming convention but is not a CRUD model api.
whenItApplies: Files whose basename appears in `NON_CRUD_API_BASENAMES` (e.g. `development.api.ts`).
whenItDoesNotApply: CRUD-style api files — those run the full structural pass.
canonicalFix: Either rename the file (drop `.api.ts`) or accept the warning — structural checks are intentionally short-circuited.

title: A field on a `*Params` interface is missing the `readonly` modifier
whatItFlags: A field on a `*Params` interface is missing the `readonly` modifier.
whenItApplies: Every property on a `*Params` interface (type aliases are skipped).
whenItDoesNotApply: Fields whose type system needs mutability (rare — params are immutable by convention).
canonicalFix: Add `readonly` to the property so the params shape stays immutable.

title: A `*ParamsType` validator declares a `Maybe<...>` field without `clearable(...)`
whatItFlags: A `*ParamsType` validator declares a `Maybe<...>` field without `clearable(...)`.
whenItApplies: When the params interface field is wrapped in `Maybe<T>` but the validator's matching property doesn't call `clearable(...)`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Fields that intentionally accept `null` / `undefined` without the clearable wrapper (rare — convention is to use `clearable`).
canonicalFix: Wrap the validator property's value with `clearable(...)` so optional / clearable fields decode correctly at runtime.

title: A `*Params` interface has no matching `*ParamsType` Arktype validator
whatItFlags: A `*Params` interface has no matching `*ParamsType` Arktype validator.
whenItApplies: Every `*Params` declaration referenced by the CRUD config.
whenItDoesNotApply: Params that are intentionally unvalidated (rare — every public CRUD entry needs a runtime validator).
canonicalFix: Add `export const <foo>ParamsType = type({ ... }) as unknown as RuntimeTypeValidator<<Foo>Params>;` adjacent to the interface.

title: A `*Params` declaration exists but isn't `export`ed
whatItFlags: A `*Params` declaration exists but isn't `export`ed.
whenItApplies: Any `*Params` interface or type alias declared without the `export` keyword.
whenItDoesNotApply: Internal helper params that aren't part of the CRUD config (rare — most params are referenced by the call map).
canonicalFix: Add the `export` keyword so the params can be referenced by downstream packages and the validator's cast.

title: A `*ParamsType` validator isn't declared adjacent to its `*Params` interface
whatItFlags: A `*ParamsType` validator isn't declared adjacent to its `*Params` interface.
whenItApplies: When the validator and its params interface are separated by another top-level declaration.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files where the validator must live in a different region for project-specific reasons.
canonicalFix: Move the validator immediately after its params interface so the pair stays grouped.

title: A `*ParamsType` validator declaration exists but isn't `export`ed
whatItFlags: A `*ParamsType` validator declaration exists but isn't `export`ed.
whenItApplies: Any `*ParamsType` constant declared without the `export` keyword.
whenItDoesNotApply: No known false-positive cases — validators are consumed by the runtime CRUD wiring.
canonicalFix: Add the `export` keyword so the validator can be referenced by the runtime wiring.

title: A `*ParamsType` validator is cast to the wrong target type
whatItFlags: A `*ParamsType` validator is cast to the wrong target type.
whenItApplies: When the validator's `as ... <Type>` cast doesn't reference the matching `*Params` interface.
whenItDoesNotApply: No known false-positive cases — the cast is what links the validator to the params interface at the type level.
canonicalFix: Cast the validator to `RuntimeTypeValidator<<Foo>Params>` (or `as unknown as RuntimeTypeValidator<<Foo>Params>`) so the CRUD config infers the right shape.

title: A field on a `*Result` interface is missing the `readonly` modifier
whatItFlags: A field on a `*Result` interface is missing the `readonly` modifier.
whenItApplies: Every property on a `*Result` interface (type aliases are skipped).
whenItDoesNotApply: Fields whose type system needs mutability (rare — results are immutable by convention).
canonicalFix: Add `readonly` to the property so the result shape stays immutable.

title: `<group>FunctionTypeConfigMap` is annotated with the wrong generic argument
whatItFlags: `<group>FunctionTypeConfigMap` is annotated with the wrong generic argument.
whenItApplies: When the type annotation isn't `ModelFirebaseFunctionMapTypeConfigMap<<Group>FunctionTypeMap>`.
whenItDoesNotApply: No known false-positive cases — the generic ties the config map to the type map by design.
canonicalFix: Set the annotation to `ModelFirebaseFunctionMapTypeConfigMap<<Group>FunctionTypeMap>` matching the file's type-map name.

title: Required `<name>.action.ts` server-actions module is missing
whatItFlags: Required `<name>.action.ts` server-actions module is missing.
whenItApplies: Every canonical model folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Reserved folders that don't follow the 5-file convention.
canonicalFix: Add `<name>.action.ts` declaring the abstract `<Model>ServerActions` class plus the action-context interface.

title: Required `<name>.api.ts` CRUD api module is missing
whatItFlags: Required `<name>.api.ts` CRUD api module is missing.
whenItApplies: Every canonical model folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Reserved folders that don't follow the 5-file convention.
canonicalFix: Add `<name>.api.ts` declaring the params/result interfaces, validators, and Functions block.

title: Required `<name>.id.ts` id-types module is missing
whatItFlags: Required `<name>.id.ts` id-types module is missing.
whenItApplies: Every canonical model folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Reserved folders that don't follow the 5-file convention.
canonicalFix: Add `<name>.id.ts` exporting the model's id alias types and any composite-key helpers.

title: Required `index.ts` barrel is missing
whatItFlags: Required `index.ts` barrel is missing.
whenItApplies: Every canonical model folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Reserved folders that don't follow the 5-file convention.
canonicalFix: Add `index.ts` re-exporting every sibling `<name>.*.ts` module so the folder is consumed through one barrel.

title: Required `<name>.ts` main module is missing from the model folder
whatItFlags: Required `<name>.ts` main module is missing from the model folder.
whenItApplies: Every canonical model folder anchored on its folder basename.
whenItDoesNotApply: Reserved folders (`system`, `notification`, `storagefile`) — they have a different layout and surface a separate `RESERVED_MODEL_FOLDER` warning instead.
canonicalFix: Add `<name>.ts` containing the model's `firestoreModelIdentity`, interface, document class, converter, and collection wiring.

title: Required `<name>.query.ts` query-helpers module is missing
whatItFlags: Required `<name>.query.ts` query-helpers module is missing.
whenItApplies: Every canonical model folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Reserved folders that don't follow the 5-file convention.
canonicalFix: Add `<name>.query.ts` with the model's query-builder helpers.

title: The path resolves to a file or other non-directory entry
whatItFlags: The path resolves to a file or other non-directory entry.
whenItApplies: Validating a path that exists but is not a directory.
whenItDoesNotApply: File-level model validation — use `dbx_model_validate` for `.ts` source files.
canonicalFix: Pass the model folder (e.g. `apps/.../src/lib/model/profile/`), not the `<name>.ts` file inside it.

title: Folder does not exist on disk
whatItFlags: Folder does not exist on disk.
whenItApplies: Validating a folder path that doesn't resolve to a directory entry.
whenItDoesNotApply: Glob patterns that intentionally match zero folders — pass at least one valid path.
canonicalFix: Verify the path argument is a real folder under the workspace.

title: A `.ts` file in the folder doesn't start with the folder's `<name>.` prefix
whatItFlags: A `.ts` file in the folder doesn't start with the folder's `<name>.` prefix.
whenItApplies: Any `.ts` file that is not `index.ts` and is not prefixed with `<folder-name>.`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files outside the canonical layout that the project intentionally hosts here (rare — prefer moving them out).
canonicalFix: Rename the file to `<folder-name>.<sub>.ts` so it stays grouped, or move it out of the model folder.

title: The folder name matches a reserved group (system / notification / storagefile)
whatItFlags:
The folder name matches a reserved group (system / notification / storagefile)
that follows a distinct layout. Validation skips it and points at the
dedicated tool.
whenItApplies: Folders named `system`, `notification`, or `storagefile`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Custom downstream folders that happen to share a substring — match is exact.
canonicalFix: Re-run validation with the recommended tool from the violation's message (e.g. `dbx_system_m_validate_folder`).

title: The API app directory does not exist on disk
whatItFlags: The API app directory does not exist on disk.
whenItApplies: When the supplied `apiDir` does not resolve to a directory entry.
whenItDoesNotApply: API apps intentionally not validated — pass `--skip notification_m` instead.
canonicalFix: Verify the path argument resolves to an existing API app root.

title: The API root exists but `src/app/common/model/notification/` is missing
whatItFlags: The API root exists but `src/app/common/model/notification/` is missing.
whenItApplies: API apps expected to wire the notification module but with no notification folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: API apps that intentionally don't wire notifications — pass `--skip notification_m`.
canonicalFix: Add `src/app/common/model/notification/` with `notification.module.ts`, `notification.task.service.ts`, and `notification.send.service.ts`.

title: The component package directory does not exist on disk
whatItFlags: The component package directory does not exist on disk.
whenItApplies: When the supplied `componentDir` does not resolve to a directory entry.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components intentionally not part of this validation pass — pass `--skip notification_m` to `dbx_app_validate` instead.
canonicalFix: Verify the path argument resolves to an existing component package root.

title: The component root exists but `src/lib/model/notification/` is missing
whatItFlags: The component root exists but `src/lib/model/notification/` is missing.
whenItApplies: Components that should ship template / task constants but lack the notification model folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that intentionally don't extend the notification group.
canonicalFix: Add `src/lib/model/notification/notification.ts` and the rest of the notification artefacts.

title: A `NotificationTaskServiceTaskHandlerConfig`'s binding name doesn't match its handler-array reference
whatItFlags: A `NotificationTaskServiceTaskHandlerConfig`'s binding name doesn't match its handler-array reference.
whenItApplies: When the handler is bound to a typed const but the local-variable name diverges from the array element name in `notificationTaskService({ handlers })`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Inline / anonymous handler literals (no binding name to check against).
canonicalFix: Rename the binding to match the handler-array element, or update the array reference to match the binding.

title: A `NotificationTaskServiceTaskHandlerConfig` references a `type:` identifier that is not a declared task-type constant
whatItFlags: A `NotificationTaskServiceTaskHandlerConfig` references a `type:` identifier that is not a declared task-type constant.
whenItApplies: When the handler's `type:` is an identifier outside the component's declared constants and the trust list.
whenItDoesNotApply: Handler configs whose `type:` is imported from a trust-listed module.
canonicalFix: Either declare the matching task-type constant or remove the orphaned handler config.

title: The `handlers:` array spreads an identifier that doesn't resolve to a declared array binding
whatItFlags: The `handlers:` array spreads an identifier that doesn't resolve to a declared array binding.
whenItApplies: When `notificationTaskService({ handlers: [...spread] })` spreads an identifier that isn't a typed `NotificationTaskServiceTaskHandlerConfig[]` binding or trusted external.
whenItDoesNotApply: Spreads from trust-listed `@dereekb/*` modules (e.g. `storageFileProcessingNotificationTaskHandler`).
canonicalFix: Declare the spread as `const <name>: NotificationTaskServiceTaskHandlerConfig<...>[] = [...]` or import it from a trusted module.

title: A task type appears in `validate:` but has no matching `handlers:` registration
whatItFlags: A task type appears in `validate:` but has no matching `handlers:` registration.
whenItApplies: When `notificationTaskService({ validate, handlers })` lists a task type in `validate:` that the `handlers:` traversal doesn't reach.
whenItDoesNotApply: Tasks whose handler is provided by an upstream module (covered by the trust list).
canonicalFix: Add the matching `NotificationTaskServiceTaskHandlerConfig` for the task and include it in `handlers:`.

title: Multiple `notificationTaskService({ ... })` calls were found
whatItFlags: Multiple `notificationTaskService({ ... })` calls were found.
whenItApplies: When more than one task-service factory exists in the API.
whenItDoesNotApply: Apps that intentionally compose multiple task services (rare — only the first call's config takes effect at runtime).
canonicalFix: Consolidate the calls into one — the second call's handlers are unused at runtime.

title: A declared `*_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPE` is not present in any `ALL_*_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPES` aggregate array
whatItFlags: A declared `*_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPE` is not present in any `ALL_*_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPES` aggregate array.
whenItApplies: Every declared task-type constant.
whenItDoesNotApply: Task-type constants intentionally held outside any aggregate (rare — most apps centralise the list).
canonicalFix: Add the constant to `ALL_<NAME>_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPES: NotificationTaskType[]` so the aggregate stays comprehensive.

title: A declared `*_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPE` is not reachable from any `notificationTaskService({ handlers })` call
whatItFlags: A declared `*_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPE` is not reachable from any `notificationTaskService({ handlers })` call.
whenItApplies: Every declared task-type constant.
whenItDoesNotApply: Task-type constants whose handler is provided by an upstream module (covered by the trust list).
canonicalFix: Add a `NotificationTaskServiceTaskHandlerConfig<...>` for the task and include its binding in `notificationTaskService({ handlers: [...] })`.

title: No `notificationTaskService({ ... })` call was found in the API
whatItFlags: No `notificationTaskService({ ... })` call was found in the API.
whenItApplies: APIs that declare at least one `*_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPE`.
whenItDoesNotApply: APIs whose task service is provided by an upstream module (rare — extend the scanner's subpaths if so).
canonicalFix: Add `notificationTaskService({ validate: [...], handlers: [...] })` and bind it through the notification module.

title: Two `*_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPE` constants share the same string literal value
whatItFlags: Two `*_NOTIFICATION_TASK_TYPE` constants share the same string literal value.
whenItApplies: When more than one task-type constant resolves to the same `'<value>'` literal.
whenItDoesNotApply: Constants intentionally aliased — but this defeats runtime task discrimination, so almost always a copy-paste.
canonicalFix: Rename one of the literals so each task code is unique.

title: No top-level `<app>NotificationTemplateServiceConfigsArrayFactory` was found
whatItFlags: No top-level `<app>NotificationTemplateServiceConfigsArrayFactory` was found.
whenItApplies: APIs that declare at least one `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo`.
whenItDoesNotApply: APIs whose configs-array factory is provided by an upstream module (rare — convention is one factory per app).
canonicalFix: Declare `export function <app>NotificationTemplateServiceConfigsArrayFactory(context): NotificationTemplateServiceTypeConfig[] { return [<foo>NotificationTemplateFactory(context), ...]; }`.

title: A `*_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE` constant has no matching `NotificationTemplateServiceTypeConfig` reachable from the configs-array factory
whatItFlags: A `*_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE` constant has no matching `NotificationTemplateServiceTypeConfig` reachable from the configs-array factory.
whenItApplies: Every declared template-type constant.
whenItDoesNotApply: Template types whose handler config is provided by an upstream module (covered by the trust list).
canonicalFix: Declare a `<foo>NotificationTemplateFactory` returning the `NotificationTemplateServiceTypeConfig` and call it from the app's configs-array factory.

title: The configs-array factory exists but no NestJS provider binds it via `NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_SERVICE_CONFIGS_ARRAY_TOKEN`
whatItFlags: The configs-array factory exists but no NestJS provider binds it via `NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_SERVICE_CONFIGS_ARRAY_TOKEN`.
whenItApplies: When `<app>NotificationTemplateServiceConfigsArrayFactory` exists but no `{ provide: NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_SERVICE_CONFIGS_ARRAY_TOKEN, useFactory }` provider exists.
whenItDoesNotApply: APIs that wire the factory indirectly through an imported app-module (rare — extend the scanner's subpaths if so).
canonicalFix: Add a NestJS provider with `provide: NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_SERVICE_CONFIGS_ARRAY_TOKEN, useFactory: <app>NotificationTemplateServiceConfigsArrayFactory`.

title: A `NotificationTemplateServiceTypeConfig` references a `type:` identifier that is not a declared template-type constant
whatItFlags: A `NotificationTemplateServiceTypeConfig` references a `type:` identifier that is not a declared template-type constant.
whenItApplies: When the handler config's `type:` is an identifier outside the component's declared constants and the trust list.
whenItDoesNotApply: Handler configs whose `type:` is imported from a trust-listed module.
canonicalFix: Either declare the matching template-type constant or remove the orphaned config.

title: The configs-array factory spreads an identifier that doesn't resolve to a declared function
whatItFlags: The configs-array factory spreads an identifier that doesn't resolve to a declared function.
whenItApplies: When `<app>NotificationTemplateServiceConfigsArrayFactory` spreads (`...fooNotifications(context)`) a function that isn't declared locally or imported.
whenItDoesNotApply: Spreads from trust-listed `@dereekb/*` modules.
canonicalFix: Declare the missing function or import it from a trusted module.

title: A `*_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE` constant has no matching `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` object
whatItFlags: A `*_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE` constant has no matching `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` object.
whenItApplies: Every declared template-type constant in the component.
whenItDoesNotApply: Template types declared in an upstream `@dereekb/*` module — those are detected via the trust list and skipped.
canonicalFix: Declare `export const <foo>NotificationTemplateTypeInfo: NotificationTemplateTypeInfo = { type: <CONSTANT>, name, description, ... }` adjacent to the constant.

title: A `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` object is missing the `name` and/or `description` field
whatItFlags: A `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` object is missing the `name` and/or `description` field.
whenItApplies: Every declared `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` whose `name` or `description` is empty / missing.
whenItDoesNotApply: Infos whose human-readable fields are intentionally blank (rare — most consumers surface them in admin UI).
canonicalFix: Populate `name: '...'` and `description: '...'` on the info object so admin / tooling surfaces have something to render.

title: A declared `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` is not reachable from the metadata aggregator record
whatItFlags: A declared `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` is not reachable from the metadata aggregator record.
whenItApplies: Every info object whose symbol does not appear in the resolved set traced from `notificationTemplateTypeInfoRecord([...])`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Infos intentionally excluded from the runtime record (rare — usually a missed registration step).
canonicalFix: Add the info symbol (or its containing `ALL_*_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE_INFOS` aggregate) to the record array.

title: A `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` object's `type:` does not match a declared template-type constant
whatItFlags: A `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` object's `type:` does not match a declared template-type constant.
whenItApplies: When the info's `type:` references a missing constant or an identifier outside the trust list.
whenItDoesNotApply: Info objects whose `type:` is provided by an imported `@dereekb/*` constant — covered by the trust list.
canonicalFix: Either declare the missing template-type constant or update the info's `type:` to a real constant in the component.

title: A declared `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` is unused — its symbol isn't reachable from the metadata aggregator
whatItFlags: A declared `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo` is unused — its symbol isn't reachable from the metadata aggregator.
whenItApplies: When the info's symbol does not appear in any aggregate array or the resolved record set.
whenItDoesNotApply: Infos kept around for documentation / future wiring (rare — usually rename drift).
canonicalFix: Either remove the orphan info or add it to the appropriate `ALL_*_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE_INFOS` aggregate.

title: No top-level `notificationTemplateTypeInfoRecord([...])` call was found
whatItFlags: No top-level `notificationTemplateTypeInfoRecord([...])` call was found.
whenItApplies: Components that declare at least one `NotificationTemplateTypeInfo`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components whose record is provided by an upstream `@dereekb/*` module (rare — convention is one record per app).
canonicalFix: Declare `export const <APP>_FIREBASE_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE_INFO_RECORD = notificationTemplateTypeInfoRecord([...]);` covering every template info.

title: The metadata record exists but is not wired through `appNotificationTemplateTypeInfoRecordService(...)` in the API
whatItFlags: The metadata record exists but is not wired through `appNotificationTemplateTypeInfoRecordService(...)` in the API.
whenItApplies: When `appNotificationTemplateTypeInfoRecordService(...)` is missing or its argument doesn't resolve to the declared record.
whenItDoesNotApply: APIs that wire the record indirectly through an imported app-module (rare — extend the scanner's subpaths if so).
canonicalFix: Add `appNotificationTemplateTypeInfoRecordService(<APP>_FIREBASE_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE_INFO_RECORD)` to the API's notification module.

title: The metadata record's `[...]` array spreads an identifier that doesn't resolve to a declared aggregate
whatItFlags: The metadata record's `[...]` array spreads an identifier that doesn't resolve to a declared aggregate.
whenItApplies: When `notificationTemplateTypeInfoRecord([...])` spreads a name that isn't a `ALL_*_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE_INFOS` aggregate or trusted external.
whenItDoesNotApply: Spreads from trust-listed `@dereekb/*` modules.
canonicalFix: Declare the aggregate as `export const ALL_<NAME>_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE_INFOS: NotificationTemplateTypeInfo[] = [...]` or import it from a trusted module.

title: Two `*_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE` constants share the same string literal value
whatItFlags: Two `*_NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_TYPE` constants share the same string literal value.
whenItApplies: When more than one template-type constant resolves to the same `'<value>'` literal.
whenItDoesNotApply: Constants intentionally aliased — but this defeats persisted-document discrimination, so almost always a copy-paste.
canonicalFix: Rename one of the literals so each template code is unique.

title: The API app directory does not exist on disk
whatItFlags: The API app directory does not exist on disk.
whenItApplies: When the supplied `apiDir` does not resolve to a directory entry.
whenItDoesNotApply: API apps intentionally not validated — pass `--skip notification_m` instead.
canonicalFix: Verify the path argument resolves to an existing API app root.

title: The API root exists but `src/app/common/model/notification/` is missing
whatItFlags: The API root exists but `src/app/common/model/notification/` is missing.
whenItApplies: API apps expected to wire the notification module but with no notification folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: API apps that intentionally don't wire notifications — pass `--skip notification_m`.
canonicalFix: Add `src/app/common/model/notification/` with `notification.module.ts`, `notification.task.service.ts`, and `notification.send.service.ts`.

title: `index.ts` re-exports a path that does not resolve to a sibling file or subfolder
whatItFlags: `index.ts` re-exports a path that does not resolve to a sibling file or subfolder.
whenItApplies: When `export * from './X'` in `index.ts` has no matching `./X.ts` or `./X/` under the notification folder root.
whenItDoesNotApply: Re-exports that intentionally point outside the folder (use a relative path that climbs out, not `./X`).
canonicalFix: Add the missing `<name>.ts` / `<name>/` sibling, or remove the dangling re-export from `index.ts`.

title: The component package directory does not exist on disk
whatItFlags: The component package directory does not exist on disk.
whenItApplies: When the supplied `componentDir` does not resolve to a directory entry.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components intentionally not part of this validation pass — pass `--skip notification_m` to `dbx_app_validate` instead.
canonicalFix: Verify the path argument resolves to an existing component package root.

title: The component root exists but `src/lib/model/notification/` is missing
whatItFlags: The component root exists but `src/lib/model/notification/` is missing.
whenItApplies: Components that should ship `NotificationTemplateType` / `NotificationTaskType` constants but lack the notification model folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that intentionally don't extend the notification group (rare — most apps with notifications do).
canonicalFix: Add `src/lib/model/notification/notification.ts` and the rest of the notification artefacts.

title: A non-canonical `.ts` file lives at the API notification root alongside a `handlers/` subfolder
whatItFlags: A non-canonical `.ts` file lives at the API notification root alongside a `handlers/` subfolder.
whenItApplies: API folders that mix root-level handler files with a sibling `handlers/` subfolder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Strictly canonical files (`notification.module.ts`, `notification.task.service.ts`, `notification.send.service.ts`, plus the convention extras tracked in `CANONICAL_API_ROOT_FILES`) — those are allowed at the root.
canonicalFix: Move the handler file into `handlers/` so the convention stays consistent across the project.

title: The API notification folder is missing the required `notification.module.ts` file
whatItFlags: The API notification folder is missing the required `notification.module.ts` file.
whenItApplies: Every API notification folder that exists.
whenItDoesNotApply: The NestJS notification module is provided elsewhere in the app (rare — convention is one module per folder).
canonicalFix: Add `notification.module.ts` declaring the NestJS module that binds the notification task / send service providers.

title: The API notification folder is missing the required `notification.send.service.ts` file
whatItFlags: The API notification folder is missing the required `notification.send.service.ts` file.
whenItApplies: Every API notification folder that exists.
whenItDoesNotApply: Apps whose `NotificationSendService` factory lives elsewhere (rare — convention is one factory per folder).
canonicalFix: Add `notification.send.service.ts` exporting the `NotificationSendService` factory wired through the notification module.

title: The API notification folder is missing the required `notification.task.service.ts` file
whatItFlags: The API notification folder is missing the required `notification.task.service.ts` file.
whenItApplies: Every API notification folder that exists.
whenItDoesNotApply: Apps whose `NotificationTaskService` factory lives elsewhere (rare — convention is one factory per folder).
canonicalFix: Add `notification.task.service.ts` exporting the `notificationTaskService({ ... })` factory call.

title: A `.ts` file in the notification folder doesn't start with the `notification.` prefix
whatItFlags: A `.ts` file in the notification folder doesn't start with the `notification.` prefix.
whenItApplies: Any `.ts` file other than `index.ts` whose basename doesn't start with `notification.`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files outside the canonical layout that the project intentionally hosts here (rare — prefer moving them out).
canonicalFix: Rename the file to `notification.<sub>.ts`, or move it out of the notification folder.

title: The API app's `src/app/common/model/storagefile/` directory does not exist
whatItFlags: The API app's `src/app/common/model/storagefile/` directory does not exist.
whenItApplies: When `apiDir` resolves to a directory but the storagefile API folder is missing.
whenItDoesNotApply: API apps that intentionally don't wire storage-file purposes — pass `--skip storagefile_m` instead.
canonicalFix: Add `<apiDir>/src/app/common/model/storagefile/` with `storagefile.module.ts`, `storagefile.upload.service.ts`, and `storagefile.init.ts`.

title: The API root exists but neither `src/app/common/model/storagefile/` nor
whatItFlags:
The API root exists but neither `src/app/common/model/storagefile/` nor
`src/app/common/model/notification/` is present (the storage-file
processing handler may live in `notification/handlers/`).
whenItApplies: When the API package is present but has no storagefile or notification subfolders.
whenItDoesNotApply: API apps that don't wire storage-file or notification purposes.
canonicalFix: Add `src/app/common/model/storagefile/` and (if processing handlers are needed) `src/app/common/model/notification/handlers/`.

title: The component's `src/lib/model/storagefile/` directory does not exist
whatItFlags: The component's `src/lib/model/storagefile/` directory does not exist.
whenItApplies: When `componentDir` resolves to a directory but the storagefile model folder is missing.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that intentionally don't ship storage-file purposes — pass `--skip storagefile_m` to `dbx_app_validate` instead.
canonicalFix: Add `<componentDir>/src/lib/model/storagefile/storagefile.ts` and the rest of the storagefile artefacts.

title: The component root exists but has no `src/lib/model/storagefile/` subfolder
whatItFlags: The component root exists but has no `src/lib/model/storagefile/` subfolder.
whenItApplies: When the component package is present but has no storagefile model folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that don't extend the storagefile group.
canonicalFix: Run `dbx_artifact_scaffold artifact="storagefile-purpose"` to scaffold the folder layout.

title: Two `UploadedFileTypeIdentifier` constants share the same string
whatItFlags:
Two `UploadedFileTypeIdentifier` constants share the same string
literal value.
whenItApplies: When more than one file-type identifier constant resolves to the same `'<value>'`.
whenItDoesNotApply: File-type identifiers intentionally re-used across purposes (rare — almost always a bug).
canonicalFix: Rename one of the literals so each file-type code is unique.

title: A `*_PURPOSE` constant has no `<purpose>FileGroupIds(...)` or
whatItFlags:
A `*_PURPOSE` constant has no `<purpose>FileGroupIds(...)` or
`<purpose>StorageFileGroupIds(...)` helper function in the same
component.
whenItApplies: Validating a downstream `-firebase` component that declares at least one `StorageFilePurpose` constant.
whenItDoesNotApply: Purposes that intentionally hold no group membership (rare — most purposes belong to a workspace's group convention).
canonicalFix: Declare `export function <purpose>FileGroupIds(input): StorageFileGroupIds` adjacent to the purpose constant.

title: A purpose with subtasks has no
whatItFlags:
A purpose with subtasks has no
`StorageFileProcessingPurposeSubtaskProcessorConfig` whose `target:`
references it.
whenItApplies: Every purpose that declares a `*ProcessingSubtask` union.
whenItDoesNotApply: Purposes whose processing config is provided by an upstream module.
canonicalFix: Add a config with `target: <PURPOSE_CONST>, flow: [{ subtask: <SUBTASK_CONST>, ... }, ...]`.

title: A processing config references a `target:` identifier that is not
whatItFlags:
A processing config references a `target:` identifier that is not
any declared `StorageFilePurpose` constant.
whenItApplies: When the `target:` identifier is local but not declared.
whenItDoesNotApply: When the target is imported from a trust-listed module.
canonicalFix: Either declare the purpose or remove the orphaned config.

title: The component declares purposes with subtasks but no
whatItFlags:
The component declares purposes with subtasks but no
`storageFileProcessingNotificationTaskHandler({ processors })` call
is present in the API.
whenItApplies: When at least one purpose declares a `*ProcessingSubtask` union and `*_PROCESSING_SUBTASK` constants.
whenItDoesNotApply: Purposes that store a file but do not need processing.
canonicalFix: Add a `storageFileProcessingNotificationTaskHandler({ processors: [...] })` call and wire it into the notification-task service.

title: A `*_PROCESSING_SUBTASK` declared on a purpose's union is absent
whatItFlags:
A `*_PROCESSING_SUBTASK` declared on a purpose's union is absent
from the matching processor config's `flow:` array.
whenItApplies: Every purpose × subtask pairing.
whenItDoesNotApply: Subtasks whose processor lives in a separate, dedicated config (uncommon — usually a missed flow step).
canonicalFix: Add `{ subtask: <SUBTASK_CONST>, processor: ... }` to the config's `flow:` array.

title: Two `StorageFilePurpose` constants share the same string literal value
whatItFlags: Two `StorageFilePurpose` constants share the same string literal value.
whenItApplies: When more than one purpose constant resolves to the same `'<value>'` literal.
whenItDoesNotApply: Purposes intentionally aliased — but this defeats persisted-document discrimination, so almost always a copy-paste.
canonicalFix: Rename one of the literals so each purpose code is unique.

title: A `*_PURPOSE: StorageFilePurpose` constant has no matching
whatItFlags:
A `*_PURPOSE: StorageFilePurpose` constant has no matching
`*_UPLOADED_FILE_TYPE_IDENTIFIER` constant alongside it.
whenItApplies: Every declared `StorageFilePurpose` constant.
whenItDoesNotApply: Purposes whose file-type identifier is intentionally imported from a trust-listed `@dereekb/firebase` module.
canonicalFix: Declare `<PREFIX>_UPLOADED_FILE_TYPE_IDENTIFIER: UploadedFileTypeIdentifier = '...'` next to the purpose.

title: A declared `UploadedFileTypeIdentifier` constant has no
whatItFlags:
A declared `UploadedFileTypeIdentifier` constant has no
`StorageFileInitializeFromUploadServiceInitializer` reachable from any
`storageFileInitializeFromUploadService(...)` call.
whenItApplies: Every declared file-type identifier.
whenItDoesNotApply: File-type identifiers that are intentionally orphaned (rare — usually a typo in a binding name; see `STORAGEFILE_UPLOAD_INITIALIZER_NAME_MISMATCH`).
canonicalFix: Add an initializer with `type: <CONSTANT>` and include it in the upload-service `initializer` array.

title: A `type:` constant is declared and referenced by an initializer, but
whatItFlags:
A `type:` constant is declared and referenced by an initializer, but
the initializer's binding name doesn't match any element of the
`initializer` array — usually a rename drift.
whenItApplies: When an initializer literal exists but the wrapping factory's local-variable name diverges from the call-site binding.
whenItDoesNotApply: Initializer factories that intentionally compose multiple bindings via `...spread` (the validator handles spreads via `STORAGEFILE_UPLOAD_SERVICE_SPREAD_UNRESOLVED`).
canonicalFix: Rename the local variable to match the binding name used in the `initializer` array element / spread, or update the array.

title: An upload initializer references a `type:` identifier that is not a
whatItFlags:
An upload initializer references a `type:` identifier that is not a
declared `UploadedFileTypeIdentifier` constant in the component (or a
trust-listed external).
whenItApplies: Every initializer literal seen in the API.
whenItDoesNotApply: When the type identifier is imported from `@dereekb/firebase`/`@dereekb/firebase-server` — those are auto-trusted.
canonicalFix: Either declare the missing constant in the component or remove the initializer.

title: No `storageFileInitializeFromUploadService(...)` call was found in
whatItFlags:
No `storageFileInitializeFromUploadService(...)` call was found in
the API.
whenItApplies: APIs that declare at least one `UploadedFileTypeIdentifier` and therefore need to wire the upload service.
whenItDoesNotApply: APIs whose upload service is provided by an upstream module (and the call lives outside the scanned subpaths — extend the scanner if so).
canonicalFix: Add a factory whose return value is built from `storageFileInitializeFromUploadService({ initializer: [...] })` and bind it as a NestJS provider.

title: Multiple `storageFileInitializeFromUploadService(...)` calls were
whatItFlags:
Multiple `storageFileInitializeFromUploadService(...)` calls were
found.
whenItApplies: When more than one upload-service factory exists in the API.
whenItDoesNotApply: Apps that intentionally compose multiple factories (rare — only the first one runs).
canonicalFix: Consolidate the factories into one — only the first call's config takes effect at runtime.

title: The upload-service factory exists but no NestJS provider binds it via
whatItFlags:
The upload-service factory exists but no NestJS provider binds it via
`provide: StorageFileInitializeFromUploadService, useFactory: ...`.
