whenItApplies: When the API has at least one upload-service factory.
whenItDoesNotApply: When the binding lives in a module imported transitively from a trust-listed `@dereekb` package.
canonicalFix: Add a NestJS provider with `provide: StorageFileInitializeFromUploadService, useFactory: <factory>` to a module imported by the app.

title: A `...spread` element inside the `initializer` array does not resolve
whatItFlags:
A `...spread` element inside the `initializer` array does not resolve
to a declared array binding or factory call.
whenItApplies: When the spread identifier is local but not declared anywhere reachable.
whenItDoesNotApply: Spread identifiers from trust-listed `@dereekb/*` modules.
canonicalFix: Declare the spread as `const <name>: StorageFileInitializeFromUploadServiceInitializer[] = [...]` or import it from a trusted module.

title: The API app directory does not exist on disk
whatItFlags: The API app directory does not exist on disk.
whenItApplies: When the supplied `apiDir` does not resolve to a directory entry.
whenItDoesNotApply: API apps intentionally not validated — pass `--skip storagefile_m` instead.
canonicalFix: Verify the path argument resolves to an existing API app root.

title: The API root exists but `src/app/common/model/storagefile/` is missing
whatItFlags: The API root exists but `src/app/common/model/storagefile/` is missing.
whenItApplies: API apps expected to wire the storagefile module but with no storagefile folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: API apps that intentionally don't wire storage uploads — pass `--skip storagefile_m`.
canonicalFix: Add `src/app/common/model/storagefile/` with `storagefile.module.ts`, `storagefile.upload.service.ts`, and `storagefile.init.ts`.

title: `index.ts` re-exports a path that does not resolve to a sibling file or subfolder
whatItFlags: `index.ts` re-exports a path that does not resolve to a sibling file or subfolder.
whenItApplies: When `export * from './X'` in `index.ts` has no matching `./X.ts` or `./X/` under the storagefile folder root.
whenItDoesNotApply: Re-exports that intentionally point outside the folder (use a relative path that climbs out, not `./X`).
canonicalFix: Add the missing `<name>.ts` / `<name>/` sibling, or remove the dangling re-export from `index.ts`.

title: The component package directory does not exist on disk
whatItFlags: The component package directory does not exist on disk.
whenItApplies: When the supplied `componentDir` does not resolve to a directory entry.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components intentionally not part of this validation pass — pass `--skip storagefile_m` to `dbx_app_validate` instead.
canonicalFix: Verify the path argument resolves to an existing component package root.

title: The component root exists but `src/lib/model/storagefile/` is missing
whatItFlags: The component root exists but `src/lib/model/storagefile/` is missing.
whenItApplies: Components that should ship `StorageFilePurpose` constants but lack the storagefile model folder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Components that intentionally don't extend the storagefile group (rare — most apps with file uploads do).
canonicalFix: Run `dbx_artifact_scaffold artifact="storagefile-purpose"` to scaffold the folder, or add `src/lib/model/storagefile/storagefile.ts` by hand.

title: A non-canonical `.ts` file lives at the API storagefile root alongside a `handlers/` subfolder
whatItFlags: A non-canonical `.ts` file lives at the API storagefile root alongside a `handlers/` subfolder.
whenItApplies: API folders that mix root-level handler files with a sibling `handlers/` subfolder.
whenItDoesNotApply: Strictly canonical files (`storagefile.module.ts`, `storagefile.upload.service.ts`, `storagefile.init.ts`) — those are allowed at the root.
canonicalFix: Move the handler file into `handlers/` so the convention stays consistent across the project.

title: The API storagefile folder is missing the required `storagefile.init.ts` file
whatItFlags: The API storagefile folder is missing the required `storagefile.init.ts` file.
whenItApplies: Every API storagefile folder that exists.
whenItDoesNotApply: Apps that don't expose storagefile server-actions on the action context (rare — most do).
canonicalFix: Add `storagefile.init.ts` with the storage-file init server-actions config wired through the action context.

title: The API storagefile folder is missing the required `storagefile.module.ts` file
whatItFlags: The API storagefile folder is missing the required `storagefile.module.ts` file.
whenItApplies: Every API storagefile folder that exists.
whenItDoesNotApply: The NestJS storage-file module is provided elsewhere in the app (rare — convention is one module per folder).
canonicalFix: Add `storagefile.module.ts` declaring the NestJS module that binds the upload service provider.

title: A `.ts` file in the storagefile folder doesn't start with the `storagefile.` prefix
whatItFlags: A `.ts` file in the storagefile folder doesn't start with the `storagefile.` prefix.
whenItApplies: Any `.ts` file other than `index.ts` whose basename doesn't start with `storagefile.`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files outside the canonical layout that the project intentionally hosts here (rare — prefer moving them out).
canonicalFix: Rename the file to `storagefile.<sub>.ts`, or move it out of the storagefile folder.

title: The API storagefile folder is missing the required `storagefile.upload.service.ts` file
whatItFlags: The API storagefile folder is missing the required `storagefile.upload.service.ts` file.
whenItApplies: Every API storagefile folder that exists.
whenItDoesNotApply: API apps whose upload service is provided by an upstream module imported transitively (rare — usually a missed scaffold step).
canonicalFix: Add `storagefile.upload.service.ts` exporting the `storageFileInitializeFromUploadService(...)` factory function.

title: The aggregate `<app>SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap` is not the last top-level export in `system.ts`
whatItFlags: The aggregate `<app>SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap` is not the last top-level export in `system.ts`.
whenItApplies: When the converter-map declaration appears before another top-level export in `system.ts`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files where the convention isn't followed for project-specific reasons — but it makes scanning the aggregator harder.
canonicalFix: Move the `<app>SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap` declaration to the end of `system.ts` so it always sits below the triples it aggregates.

title: A file disallowed at the system folder root is present (`system.id.ts` or `system.query.ts`)
whatItFlags: A file disallowed at the system folder root is present (`system.id.ts` or `system.query.ts`).
whenItApplies: When the folder contains `system.id.ts` (SystemState identifiers are plain string keys) or `system.query.ts` (documents are fetched by key, not queried).
whenItDoesNotApply: No known false-positive cases — the listed files conflict with the SystemState convention.
canonicalFix: Delete the disallowed file. The reason is included in the violation message.

title: Required `index.ts` barrel is missing from the system folder
whatItFlags: Required `index.ts` barrel is missing from the system folder.
whenItApplies: Every system folder that resolves to an existing directory.
whenItDoesNotApply: The base `@dereekb/firebase` system folder — covered above.
canonicalFix: Add `index.ts` re-exporting `system.ts` (and any optional siblings) so the folder is consumed through one barrel.

title: Required `system.ts` main module is missing from the system folder
whatItFlags: Required `system.ts` main module is missing from the system folder.
whenItApplies: Every system folder that resolves to an existing directory.
whenItDoesNotApply: The base `@dereekb/firebase` system folder — that ships machinery (`systemStateIdentity` etc.) and should not be validated with this tool.
canonicalFix: Add `system.ts` declaring the downstream `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constants, `<Foo>SystemData` interfaces, converters, and the aggregate `<app>SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap`.

title: The supplied path resolves to a file or other non-directory entry
whatItFlags: The supplied path resolves to a file or other non-directory entry.
whenItApplies: When the path exists but is not a directory.
whenItDoesNotApply: File-level system validation — pass the folder, not `system.ts`.
canonicalFix: Pass the system folder (e.g. `apps/.../src/lib/model/system/`), not the `system.ts` file inside it.

title: The supplied path does not exist on disk
whatItFlags: The supplied path does not exist on disk.
whenItApplies: When the folder path argument does not resolve to an entry.
whenItDoesNotApply: Glob patterns intentionally matching zero folders — pass at least one valid path.
canonicalFix: Verify the path resolves to an existing system model folder under the workspace.

title: A `.ts` file in the system folder doesn't follow the `system.<sub>.ts` convention
whatItFlags: A `.ts` file in the system folder doesn't follow the `system.<sub>.ts` convention.
whenItApplies: Any `.ts` file other than `index.ts` whose basename doesn't start with `system.`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Files outside the canonical layout that the project intentionally hosts here (rare — prefer moving them out).
canonicalFix: Rename the file to `system.<sub>.ts`, or move it out of the system folder.

title: A `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` / `<Foo>SystemData` pair has no matching `<foo>SystemDataConverter`
whatItFlags: A `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` / `<Foo>SystemData` pair has no matching `<foo>SystemDataConverter`.
whenItApplies: Every state-type constant whose name normalizes to a `<Foo>SystemData` interface but no `<foo>SystemDataConverter` constant exists.
whenItDoesNotApply: Converters provided by an upstream package and re-exported through `system.ts` (rare — usually a missed scaffold step).
canonicalFix: Add `export const <foo>SystemDataConverter: SystemStateStoredDataFieldConverterConfig<<Foo>SystemData> = { ... }` adjacent to the interface.

title: `system.ts` declares state-type triples but no aggregate `<app>SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap` is exported
whatItFlags: `system.ts` declares state-type triples but no aggregate `<app>SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap` is exported.
whenItApplies: When `system.ts` contains at least one `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` / `<Foo>SystemData` / `<foo>SystemDataConverter` triple.
whenItDoesNotApply: `system.ts` files that declare zero triples (an empty downstream system folder is still valid as long as `system.ts` exists).
canonicalFix: Add `export const <app>SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap: SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap = { ... }` mapping each `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` to its converter.

title: A `<Foo>SystemData` interface has no matching `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant
whatItFlags: A `<Foo>SystemData` interface has no matching `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant.
whenItApplies: Every `<Foo>SystemData` interface declared in `system.ts`.
whenItDoesNotApply: Interfaces provided by an upstream package and re-exported here (rare — usually a missed scaffold step).
canonicalFix: Add `export const <NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE: SystemStateType = '<value>'` adjacent to the interface.

title: A `<foo>SystemDataConverter` exists with no matching `<Foo>SystemData` interface or `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant
whatItFlags: A `<foo>SystemDataConverter` exists with no matching `<Foo>SystemData` interface or `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant.
whenItApplies: When the converter's prefix has no sibling interface + constant.
whenItDoesNotApply: The converter is intentionally re-exported from another package (rare — usually a typo or rename drift).
canonicalFix: Either declare the matching interface + constant pair or rename the converter so it pairs with an existing triple.

title: A `<Foo>SystemData` interface exists but no matching `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant — pair is orphaned
whatItFlags: A `<Foo>SystemData` interface exists but no matching `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant — pair is orphaned.
whenItApplies: When `<Foo>SystemData` is declared without a same-prefix state-type constant.
whenItDoesNotApply: The interface is intentionally re-exported from another package (rare — usually a typo or rename drift).
canonicalFix: Either declare the matching `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant or rename the interface so it pairs with an existing constant.

title: A declared `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant is not referenced by the aggregate converter map
whatItFlags: A declared `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant is not referenced by the aggregate converter map.
whenItApplies: Every state-type constant declared in `system.ts`.
whenItDoesNotApply: State types provided by upstream packages (the validator only reports locally declared constants).
canonicalFix: Add the constant as a key on `<app>SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap` so the runtime knows which converter to use.

title: The aggregate converter map references a key that is not a declared state-type constant in `system.ts`
whatItFlags: The aggregate converter map references a key that is not a declared state-type constant in `system.ts`.
whenItApplies: When a key on `<app>SystemStateStoredDataConverterMap` is neither a local `<NAME>_SYSTEM_STATE_TYPE` constant nor an imported identifier.
whenItDoesNotApply: Keys imported from upstream packages — those are detected via `importedIdentifiers` and skipped.
canonicalFix: Either declare the missing constant locally or import it from the upstream package that owns it.
